titleStmt.title: Letter from H A Thomas to Nicholas Lamb, 3 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: H A Thomas

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from H A Thomas to Nicholas Lamb asking for an allocation of emergency funds for the restoration of a collapsed gas tank. Nicholas Lamb (b. 1861) was the Town Clerk of Longford and served on the Longford Urban District Council. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002. H. A. Thomas worked in the Longford Gas Works.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Gas Works
Longford 3 rd :3:16 N Lamb
Town Hall Dear Sir — In order to meet an emergency
which has arisen in connection with my Gas Holder
tank I am in urgent need of about 80,000 gallons
of water. My tank has fallen about 4tr below
water level and in order to enable me to tide over
the difficulty and bring me on to the time when it
would better suit me to let down the holder I would
deem it a great favor if your Council could see
its way to letting me have the above mentioned
quantity of water from their supply at as
reasonable a rate as possible.
I might add that providing the leak does
increase and I have no reason to think that it should
I estimate that my own water pump from the river
ought enable me keep pace with the leakage -
I would also feel greatly obliged if your Council
could see its way towards giving my application
its very earliest attention as it is a matter of
urgency Yours questionable reading
H A Thomas
Manager
